verses.text: At Corinth Paul lodges with Aquila and Priscilla, tentmakers like himself. Working apostle. He labours with his hands while he preaches.
At Corinth Paul lodges with Aquila and Priscilla, tentmakers like himself. Working apostle. He labours with his hands while he preaches.
At Corinth Paul lodges with Aquila and Priscilla, tentmakers like himself. Working apostle. He labours with his hands while he preaches.
When they oppose, 'Your blood be upon your own heads... I will go to the Gentiles.' Cleared conscience. He turns to those who will hear.
When they oppose, 'Your blood be upon your own heads... I will go to the Gentiles.' Cleared conscience. He turns to those who will hear.
When they oppose, 'Your blood be upon your own heads... I will go to the Gentiles.' Cleared conscience. He turns to those who will hear.
Many Corinthians believe and are baptized. Fruit at Corinth. The gospel takes root in a sinful city.
Many Corinthians believe and are baptized. Fruit at Corinth. The gospel takes root in a sinful city.
The Lord says, 'Be not afraid... I have much people in this city'; he stays a year and a half. Encouraged. God's promise steadies His servant.
The Lord says, 'Be not afraid... I have much people in this city'; he stays a year and a half. Encouraged. God's promise steadies His servant.
The Lord says, 'Be not afraid... I have much people in this city'; he stays a year and a half. Encouraged. God's promise steadies His servant.
The Jews bring Paul before Gallio, accusing him. Dragged to court. They try the law against the gospel.
The Jews bring Paul before Gallio, accusing him. Dragged to court. They try the law against the gospel.
Gallio 'cared for none of those things' and drives them away. Case dismissed. God restrains the foe by an indifferent judge.
Gallio 'cared for none of those things' and drives them away. Case dismissed. God restrains the foe by an indifferent judge.
Gallio 'cared for none of those things' and drives them away. Case dismissed. God restrains the foe by an indifferent judge.
Gallio 'cared for none of those things' and drives them away. Case dismissed. God restrains the foe by an indifferent judge.
Paul sails to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla, having taken a vow. On the move. The apostle keeps travelling and serving.
Paul sails to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla, having taken a vow. On the move. The apostle keeps travelling and serving.
He goes through Galatia and Phrygia, 'strengthening all the disciples.' Building up. He revisits the churches to confirm them.
He goes through Galatia and Phrygia, 'strengthening all the disciples.' Building up. He revisits the churches to confirm them.
He goes through Galatia and Phrygia, 'strengthening all the disciples.' Building up. He revisits the churches to confirm them.
He goes through Galatia and Phrygia, 'strengthening all the disciples.' Building up. He revisits the churches to confirm them.
Apollos, 'eloquent... and mighty in the scriptures,' knows only John's baptism; Aquila and Priscilla teach him 'more perfectly.' Humble learner. The gifted man is glad to learn more.
Apollos, 'eloquent... and mighty in the scriptures,' knows only John's baptism; Aquila and Priscilla teach him 'more perfectly.' Humble learner. The gifted man is glad to learn more.
Apollos, 'eloquent... and mighty in the scriptures,' knows only John's baptism; Aquila and Priscilla teach him 'more perfectly.' Humble learner. The gifted man is glad to learn more.
He helps the brethren much, 'mightily convinced the Jews... that Jesus was Christ.' Powerful preacher. His learning serves the gospel.
